steps.title: Check current backstage setup
steps.description:
Verify your backstage version and configuration:
```bash
npx backstage-cli info
```
This feature requires a working backstage installation.

steps.title: Review backstage configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant backstage configuration:
**Status:** Closed

## Need

Backstage is heading out of alpha and on to a path to an eventual long-term stable release and version `1.0`.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [RFC] Stability Index
steps.description:
Some open questions:

- Should we document a more strict process around updating stabilities? The plan in the core team is to target different parts of the project and then update the stability when we feel we're ready to commit to higher stability. Wondering if it's useful with a bit of transparency or if that's not generally interesting to most people reading this doc.
- How do we handle plugins not owned by the core team, should they be included at all, perhaps opt-in from authors? Right now

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "[RFC] Stability Index" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Some open questions:

- Should we document a more strict process around updating stabilities? The plan in the core team is to target different parts of the project and then update the stability when we feel we're ready to commit to higher stability. Wondering if it's useful with a bit of transparency or if that's not generally interesting to most people reading this doc.

prerequisites.description: A working backstage installation or development environment.
